senses.definition: a brand of a product, particularly a car

examples.x: Ferrari is a renowned marque in luxury sports cars.
Each marque at the auto show showcased unique innovations.
The Bentley marque represents unparalleled elegance.
Collectors often seek out rare marques.
The brand's new model strengthens its marque's reputation.

senses.definition: the manufacturer or brand of a vehicle

examples.x: The make of the car was displayed prominently on the grille.
She owned several vehicles, each from a different make.
He researched various makes before buying his new car.
They specialized in repairing cars from a specific make.
The dealership had a wide selection of makes to choose from.
The automaker announced a recall for several models.
She worked as an engineer for a leading automaker.
e invested in stocks of a well-known automaker.
The automaker unveiled its latest electric vehicle at the auto show.
They partnered with an international automaker to expand their market.

senses.definition: a business that produces cars in large quantities by using machinery

examples.x: Toyota is a globally renowned car manufacturer known for its reliable vehicles.
The car manufacturer announced the launch of its new electric vehicle line.
She works as an engineer at a leading car manufacturer, designing innovative safety features.
The car manufacturer recalled thousands of vehicles due to a defect in the braking system.
German car manufacturers are famous for producing luxury and high-performance automobiles.

senses.definition: a section of a factory where vehicle bodies are assembled and welded together before progressing through the production line

examples.x: The body shop is where the bare metal frames of vehicles are transformed into complete car bodies through precise assembly and welding processes.
Robots are commonly used in modern body shops to ensure consistency and accuracy in welding and assembling car components.
Before entering the body shop, the stamped metal panels undergo preparation stages such as cleaning, priming, and sometimes painting.
In large automobile factories, the body shop is a critical area that requires skilled technicians and engineers to oversee the automated assembly processes.
The efficiency of the body shop directly impacts the overall production timeline of the factory, influencing the rate at which finished vehicles can be delivered to customers.

senses.definition: a production process where a product is put together in a step-by-step manner by different people or machines, each responsible for a specific task

examples.x: Cars are built on an assembly line to improve efficiency.
The factory introduced a faster assembly line last year.
Robots now assist workers on the assembly line.
The company expanded its assembly line to meet demand.
Each worker on the assembly line has a specific task.

senses.definition: a series of workers and machines in a factory where a succession of identical items is progressively assembled

examples.x: The production line was upgraded to improve efficiency.
She toured the production line to see how cars are made.
The production line operated 24/7 to meet high demand.
They automated parts of the production line to reduce costs.
The production line workers were trained in quality control.

senses.definition: to join two or more pieces of metal together using heat and pressure

examples.x: He carefully welded the steel beams to create a sturdy frame.
The blacksmith skillfully welded the broken iron gate back into place.
The automotive technician had to weld the exhaust pipe to fix the leak.
During the construction, workers needed to weld the joints of the pipeline for durability.
The engineer decided to weld the metal brackets to ensure a secure attachment.

senses.definition: a commercial space or facility where products or services are displayed or demonstrated to potential customers

examples.x: The new car showroom has a variety of models on display for customers to look at.
I went to the furniture showroom to find a new sofa for my living room.
The electronics showroom had all the latest gadgets, from smartphones to laptops.
They opened a new showroom downtown, where they offer custom-made jewelry.
Before buying the new phone, I went to the showroom to try it out first.

senses.definition: an opportunity to drive a vehicle to evaluate its performance before purchase

examples.x: He scheduled a test drive for the new model.
She was impressed by the smooth ride during the test drive.
The dealership offered a free test drive for potential buyers.
They took several cars out for a test drive before deciding.
The salesperson accompanied him on the test drive to answer questions.

senses.definition: a business authorized to sell and service vehicles from specific automakers

examples.x: The dealership had a wide range of new and used cars.
She visited the dealership to inquire about financing options.
He worked at a dealership as a sales representative.
They expanded their dealership to include more brands.
The dealership hosted an event to showcase the latest models.

senses.definition: a business that buys and sells new or used cars, often offering related services like repairs or financing

examples.x: The car dealer expanded its business by opening a new showroom downtown.
This car dealer specializes in luxury brands and high-end vehicles.
Online platforms have changed how car dealer businesses operate.
The family-owned car dealer has been in business for over 40 years.

senses.definition: the business of selling parts or accessories for vehicles after they have been bought

examples.x: Many car owners visit the aftermarket to find cheaper replacement parts.
She bought new tires from the aftermarket store down the street.
The mechanic recommended getting a high-quality aftermarket battery.
There are many aftermarket options available for customizing your car.
Aftermarket products can sometimes be better than the original ones.

senses.definition: a type of vehicle that is built from components or parts that are assembled by the buyer rather than being pre-assembled by a manufacturer
a fee that covers the cost of transporting a new vehicle from the factory to the dealership

examples.x: When buying a new car, you must pay a destination charge in addition to the price of the car.
The destination charge for my friend's new SUV was $1,200.
Many people forget to include the destination charge when calculating the total cost of a new vehicle.
The salesperson explained that the destination charge is mandatory for all new cars.
Even if the car dealership is nearby, the destination charge remains the same.
